data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Spotify employs a microservices architecture for scalable and independent service deployments.
data.technical_spec: Services are developed, deployed, and scaled independently, often using REST or gRPC APIs.

data.title: Event-Driven Design
data.description: Utilizes an event-driven system to handle asynchronous communication across services.
data.technical_spec: Employs message brokers like Kafka for event streaming.

data.title: Cloud Integration
data.description: Spotify heavily relies on cloud services for resource management and scaling.
data.technical_spec: Primarily using Google Cloud Platform for hosting and data processing needs.

data.title: Data Infrastructure
data.description: Robust data handling and processing capabilities to support millions of users.
data.technical_spec: Incorporates technologies like Hadoop, Apache Beam, and Pub/Sub for data operations.

data.title: API First Approach
data.description: Designed to enable third-party integrations and internal service communications.
data.technical_spec: Extensive use of open APIs for both internal and external accessibility.
